commit.message:
fix: import Depends from encord_agents, not fastapi

The SDK's dependency resolver does isinstance(arg, encord_agents.Depends).
Using fastapi.Depends fails that check silently, causing video_iterator
to be treated as an unsupported field param instead of a dependency.

commit.message:
Fix fitness revenue calculation - add $3,407 missing revenue

The calculate_fitness_amount function was only recognizing "Fitness Only"
and "Fitness-Only" as 100% fitness memberships. This missed:
- "Fitness Membership" (new type)
- "Fitness Annual Membership"
- "Fitness Unlimited- 3 Month"
- "Fitness Kickstart- 8 week"
- "Fitness 5 Pack" (entry pass)

These were being treated as $28 add-ons instead of 100% fitness.

Total missing fitness revenue: $3,407.21

commit.message:
[vm] mono-move: instruction set definition (#18907)

* [vm] mono-move: add instruction set definition for discussion

Initial instruction set definition for the mono-move runtime. This file
defines the bytecode instructions grouped by category (data movement,
arithmetic, control flow, vectors, references, heap objects, debugging)
with design notes on open questions: addressing modes, size specialization,
branch design, immediate representation, endianness, and encoding.

Not wired into any build yet — intended as a basis for discussion on the
compiler-runtime interface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [vm] mono-move: add license field to Cargo.toml

Fix cargo-deny failure: the license field was missing from the manifest.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "[vm] mono-move: add license field to Cargo.toml"

This reverts commit 5f4c31e80cda188b6ca8b840c93a88900c867f1d.

* [vm] mono-move: use workspace inherited keys in Cargo.toml

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [vm] mono-move: address PR review feedback on instruction set

- Rename Instruction → MicroOp, Obj* → Heap* (HeapMoveFrom/To, HeapBorrow, HeapNew)
- Rename Mov → Move, *Const → *Imm, RemU64 → ModU64, val → imm
- Add FrameOffset and CodeOffset typed wrappers
- Add RSubU64Imm (reverse subtract), StackBorrow (was BorrowLocal)
- Reorder fields: dst/target first, sources after
- Add design overview: calling convention with frame diagram, inline
  structs, highly specialized ops, fixed-size (24B) micro-ops
- Add naming conventions and object descriptor table docs
- Add micro_op_size test asserting 24-byte enum size
- Fix ForceGC (remove stack map), zero-initialize HeapNew,
  add StoreRandomU64 reasoning

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [vm] mono-move: additional refinements from review discussion

- Add overview paragraph after module title
- Move calling convention (with frame diagram) and inline structs
  from "decisions to revisit" to "design overview" (settled decisions)
- Rename Mov/Mov8 → Move/Move8 for consistency
- Rename BorrowLocal → StackBorrow (pairs with HeapBorrow)
- Add CodeOffset type, rename dst_pc → target
- Expand object descriptor table doc (Trivial/Struct/Enum/Vector
  variants, ptr_offsets naming consistent with heap_ptr convention)
- Refine StoreRandomU64 doc, fix inline struct examples

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [vm] mono-move: restructure as workspace with types subcrate

Move the mono-move crate into types/ subdirectory and rename to
mono-move-types, preparing for additional subcrates (compiler,
interpreter, etc.) under the mono-move directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [vm] mono-move: rename types subcrate to micro-ops

Rename the mono-move-types crate to mono-move-micro-ops and its
directory from types/ to micro-ops/ to better reflect its purpose.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [vm] mono-move: address review feedback on micro-ops

- Remove "from Move bytecode" from module doc (vineethk)
- Use "slots" terminology in calling convention docs (vineethk)
- Add FRAME_METADATA_SIZE constant and reference it in docs (vineethk)
- Clarify implicit vs explicit behavior for Call/Return (vineethk)
- Rename "Object descriptor table" to "Heap object descriptor table" (vineethk)
- Rename ObjectDescriptor to HeapObjectDescriptor (vineethk)
- Add note about frame slots storing raw pointers to heap (vineethk)
- Add "or enums" to heap allocation reasons (vineethk)
- Rename StackBorrow to SlotBorrow (vineethk)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [vm] mono-move: follow-up review feedback on micro-ops

- Fix CallFunc/Return doc comments to clarify implicit vs explicit
  behavior, use FRAME_METADATA_SIZE instead of hardcoded 24
- Clarify VecNew descriptor_id = 0 references the Trivial descriptor
- Add boolean branch discussion to control flow section header

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [vm] mono-move: drop "If" from conditional jump names

Rename JumpIf* to Jump* for brevity, consistent with common ISA
conventions (e.g. x86 jnz/jge/jl):
- JumpIfNotZeroU64 -> JumpNotZeroU64
- JumpIfGreaterEqualU64Imm -> JumpGreaterEqualU64Imm
- JumpIfLessU64 -> JumpLessU64

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix $0 proration bug: Snowflake Decimal * float type mismatch

Root cause: Snowflake TRY_TO_NUMBER returns Python Decimal, not float.
The proration calc `(overlap_days / total_days) * dramount` threw
"unsupported operand type(s) for *: 'float' and 'decimal.Decimal'"
which was silently swallowed by bare `except`, yielding $0 for every row.

Fix: cast dramount/cramount to float after GL aggregation.

E2E test (test_e2e.py): 20/20 passed — 498 charge rows, $35,157.42 total.
